author: The Kremlin Armory Workshops, Moscow
description: Medium: Silver, partly gilt, niello, enamel, sapphires, rubies, spinels, pearls, leather, silk velvet, oil paint, gesso, linen, mica, pig-skin, woods: Tilia cordata (basswood or linden), white oak. Dimensions: Overall (open, confirmed): 27 × 35 3/4 × 5 in. (68.6 × 90.8 × 12.7 cm). Credit: Rogers Fund, 1975
title: Triptych with the Mandylion

author: Apollonio di Giovanni di Tomaso
description: Medium: Poplar wood, linen, polychromed and gilded gesso with panel painted in tempera and gold. Dimensions: H. 39-1/2 x W. 77 x D. 32-7/8 in.  (100.3 x 195.6 x 83.5 cm); Painted surface 15 1/4 x 49 1/2 in. (38.7 x 125.7 cm). Credit: John Stewart Kennedy Fund, 1914
title: Cassone with painted front panel depicting the Conquest of Trebizond

author: Philip Webb
description: Medium: Painted pine, oil paint on leather, brass, copper. Dimensions: Overall: 73 × 45 × 21 in. (185.4 × 114.3 × 53.3 cm). Credit: Rogers Fund, 1926
title: The Backgammon Players

author: Jean Georges (or George)
description:
Medium: Gold, grisaille enamel, diamonds. Dimensions: Box: 1 3/4 x 3 3/8 x 2 5/8 in.  (4.4 x 8.6 x 6.7 cm)

Dimensions of miniatures (not sight measurements): On cover, H. 1 11/16, W. 2 9/16 in. (4.3 x 6.5 cm.); On bottom, H. 1 5/8, W. 2-1/2 in. (4.1 x 6.4 cm.); On front, H. 15/16, W. 1 7/8 in. (2.4 x 4.8 cm.); On back, H. 7/8, W. 1 7/8 in. (2.2 x 4.8 cm.); On left side, H. 13/16, W. 1 7/16 in. (2.1 x 3.7 cm.); On right side, H. 13/16, 1 7/16 in. (2.1 x 3.7 cm.).. Credit: Gift of J. Pierpont Morgan, 1917
title: Snuffbox with six scenes of putti at play

author: Mathieu Coiny fils
description: Medium: Gold, enamel, gem stones. Dimensions: Overall: 2 3/4 × 2 1/8 in. (7 × 5.4 cm). Credit: Gift of J. Pierpont Morgan, 1917
title: Snuffbox

author: Matthias Walbaum
description: Medium: Ebony, silver, gilded silver, gouache on parchment. Dimensions: Overall (confirmed): 16 15/16 × 7 1/4 × 4 1/8 in. (43 × 18.4 × 10.5 cm). Credit: Gift of J. Pierpont Morgan, 1917
title: Shrine

author: Unidentified artist
description:
Medium: Hanging scroll; ink, color, and gold on silk. Dimensions: Image: 44 3/4 × 32 5/8 in. (113.7 × 82.9 cm)
Overall with mounting: 95 1/2 × 54 in. (242.6 × 137.2 cm)
Overall with knobs: 57 1/2 × 95 1/2 in. (146.1 × 242.6 cm). Credit: Rogers Fund, 1941
title: Emperor Xuanzong's flight to Shu

author: Kano Sansetsu
description:
Medium: Four sliding-door panels (fusuma); ink, color, gold, and gold leaf on paper. Dimensions: Overall (of all four panels): 68 3/4 x 191 1/8 in. (174.6 x 485.5 cm)
Overall (a): 68 3/8 x 47 5/8 in. (173.7 x 121 cm)
Overall (b): 68 3/8 x 48 3/4 in. (173.7 x 123.8 cm)
Overall (c): 68 1/2 x 47 3/4 in. (174 x 121.3 cm)
Overall (d): 68 1/2 x 47 3/4 in. (174 x 121.3 cm). Credit: The Harry G. C. Packard Collection of Asian Art, Gift of Harry G. C. Packard, and Purchase, Fletcher, Rogers, Harris Brisbane Dick, and Louis V. Bell Funds, Joseph Pulitzer Bequest, and The Annenberg Fund Inc. Gift, 1975
title: Old Plum

author: Court of King Mindon or Thibaw, or associated workshops
description:
Medium: Watercolor and gold on mulberry paper, with gilt lacquered wood covers and cloth lined box. Dimensions: Customized box: H. 17 in. (43.2 cm); W. 7 3/4 in. (19.7 cm); D. 3 in. (7.6 cm)
Manuscript: H. 16 1/2 in. (41.9 cm); W. 7 1/4 in. (18.4 cm); D. 2 1/4 in. (5.7 cm). Credit: Purchase, Friends of Asian Art and David L. Rawle Gifts, 2017
title: Royal processions, ceremonies and entertainments

author: Joseph Etienne Blerzy
description: Medium: Gold, enamel, diamonds; ivory, glass. Dimensions: 1 3/8 x 3 1/4 x 2 1/2 in. (3.5 x 8.3 x 6.4cm); Miniature: oval, 1 1/4 x 1 in. (32 x 25 mm). Credit: Bequest of Edward C. Post, 1915
title: Snuffbox with portrait of Louis XVI (1754–1793), King of France
